frameworks.name: decision variable
frameworks.one_line: Neural signal lawfully covarying with choice, evidence, and reaction time

frameworks.name: neural population dynamics
frameworks.one_line: Single-trial DV dynamics analyzed at the population level

frameworks.name: progressive recruitment model
frameworks.one_line: Computational model recruiting units with stable choice-related modulation

frameworks.name: sequential activation in non-overlapping ensembles
frameworks.one_line: Rodent finding of short activity sequences in distinct neuronal groups

core_question: How are single-trial decision variables represented in PMd and M1 populations during perceptual choice?
key_assumptions: Decision variables can be read out from simultaneously recorded neural populations
Arm movement reports faithfully index the underlying decision
Stimulus difficulty maps onto evidence strength in the DV
manipulations: Stimulus difficulty in random dots direction discrimination
Fixed vs variable stimulus duration (temporal uncertainty)
Compared PMd vs M1 simultaneously recorded populations
key_findings: Single-trial DVs covary with stimulus difficulty in both PMd and M1
DVs are stronger and earlier in PMd than M1 under fixed stimulus duration
Temporal uncertainty accelerates DV dynamics and abolishes PMd vs M1 distinction
DV is distributed across many neurons with stable modulation, not short sequences
interpretations: Progressive recruitment of stably-modulated units accounts for population DV dynamics
Primate motor cortical decision signals differ qualitatively from rodent sequential ensembles
